pattern: Grow the longest already-sorted prefix and suffix, then two-pointer-merge across the gap to find the shortest middle chunk whose removal reconnects them.
hints: Find the longest run at the very start that is already non-decreasing, and the longest run at the very end that is already non-decreasing; anything strictly between those two runs must be removed, at least in part.
If the whole array is already one non-decreasing run, the answer is 0. Otherwise a valid removal always exists: drop everything after the prefix, or everything before the suffix.

Walk a pointer through the kept prefix and a pointer through the kept suffix together: whenever the prefix value is <= the suffix value, that combination is stitchable, so record the gap length and advance the prefix pointer; otherwise advance the suffix pointer.

edge_inputs.description: single spike in an otherwise sorted array
duplicate values straddling the prefix/suffix seam
multiple humps forcing the two-pointer walk to skip past several mismatches

stress.description: large sorted array with a random contiguous segment shuffled out of order
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    arr = sorted(rng.randint(0, 1000000000) for _ in range(n))
    if n >= 3:
        i = rng.randint(0, n - 2)
        j = rng.randint(i, n - 1)
        seg = arr[i:j + 1]
        rng.shuffle(seg)
        arr[i:j + 1] = seg
    return [arr]
stress.note: keeps the surrounding parts sorted so a real fix always exists
